what_it_does: Renders a fixed 1600x900 static presentation view with a centered title, two large cards for market metrics and relationship structure, blueprint corner underlays, a thin patterned footer strip, and a circular V mark.
observations: Uses inline CSS plus external Plus Jakarta Sans and /surface/styles/vars.css variables.
The left card shows Market metrics: +40.9%, +71.1%, and $228M SAM, followed by two short fragment rows.
The right card shows a Maker to Hub to Client relationship chain with bronze connector lines and gold arrow terminals, followed by three fragment rows.
Blueprint grid underlays appear in the top-left and top-right corners with radial masks and low opacity.
The original footer band content remains in the DOM but is hidden by a later v2.1 CSS override that compresses the footer to a thin patterned strip.
A circular V mark is absolutely positioned near the lower right and lifted by the v2.1 correction.
standouts: Two-panel composition pairing numeric market sizing with a relationship diagram.
Late correction block materially changes the footer from a content band into a quiet visual foot.
Uses repeated triangle markers and bronze/gold connector language across metric and relationship content.
conventions: Single-file HTML document with embedded CSS for the whole view.
Design intent is annotated with numbered G comments and a v2.1 correction comment.
Relies heavily on CSS custom properties from the shared vars stylesheet.
Uses fixed canvas dimensions rather than responsive layout behavior.
Keeps visual text in title case across headings and fragment rows.
concerns: The footer copy is hidden with display:none while still present in markup.
The v2.1 correction depends on !important overrides instead of removing or rewriting the earlier footer rules.
The layout is fixed at 1600x900, so behavior outside that presentation size is not defined in this file.
The content is visually structured with divs rather than semantic heading or section elements.
notes: Brief this file as a static market-and-relationship slide with a two-card metric/diagram layout and a later footer simplification override.
questions: Are the Market labels intentionally generic as Segment A and Segment B?
Should the hidden local-market footer copy remain in the DOM after the v2.1 correction?
Is the V mark a version mark, brand mark, or slide marker?
purpose_vs_actual: The stated title and filename match the actual content: the file presents market metrics alongside a relationship pathway in a v2 presentation view.
intention_signals: It appears to be trying to make market opportunity and stakeholder flow legible as one paired argument, while the correction block visually suppresses the footer copy and keeps the lower edge subdued.
novelty: The numbered design-layer comments and the late v2.1 override create a visible record of composition decisions inside the CSS.
contribution: Contributes a two-card presentation substrate that combines quantified market metrics, a compact relationship-chain diagram, blueprint texture, fragment rows, and a restrained corrected footer treatment.
summary_for_embedding: Static HTML presentation slide for counterpart-design showing market growth, SAM, and a Maker-Hub-Client relationship diagram in a fixed two-card layout.
intention_for_embedding: Designed to communicate a market opportunity and access relationship as a balanced visual composition with quiet technical underlay and restrained footer grounding.
